controlsCatalog.name: include_projected
controlsCatalog.description: When true, includes projected future-dated external cash movements (for example deposits and withdrawals).

controlsCatalog.name: security_id
controlsCatalog.description: Filter by a specific security identifier.

controlsCatalog.name: product_type
controlsCatalog.description: Filter by a specific product type such as Equity or Bond.

controlsCatalog.name: skip
controlsCatalog.description: Number of records to skip for pagination

controlsCatalog.name: limit
controlsCatalog.description: Maximum number of records to return

controlsCatalog.name: security_id
controlsCatalog.description: Security identifier for the market-price series request.

controlsCatalog.name: start_date
controlsCatalog.description: The start date for the date range filter (inclusive).

controlsCatalog.name: end_date
controlsCatalog.description: The end date for the date range filter (inclusive).

controlsCatalog.name: from_currency
controlsCatalog.description: Base currency code for the requested FX series.

controlsCatalog.name: to_currency
controlsCatalog.description: Quote currency code for the requested FX series.

controlsCatalog.name: start_date
controlsCatalog.description: The start date for the date range filter (inclusive).

controlsCatalog.name: end_date
controlsCatalog.description: The end date for the date range filter (inclusive).

controlsCatalog.name: client_id
controlsCatalog.description: Optional CIF filter for tenant/client scoping.

controlsCatalog.name: booking_center_code
controlsCatalog.description: Optional booking-center filter for business-unit specific catalogs.

controlsCatalog.name: q
controlsCatalog.description: Optional case-insensitive search text applied to portfolio ID.

controlsCatalog.name: limit
controlsCatalog.description: Maximum number of lookup items to return after filtering and sorting.

controlsCatalog.name: limit
controlsCatalog.description: Maximum number of instrument lookup items to return.

controlsCatalog.name: product_type
controlsCatalog.description: Optional product type filter (for example: Equity, Bond).

controlsCatalog.name: q
controlsCatalog.description: Optional case-insensitive search text applied to security ID and instrument name.

controlsCatalog.name: instrument_page_limit
controlsCatalog.description: Page size used when scanning instruments to derive the currency catalog.

controlsCatalog.name: source
controlsCatalog.description: Currency source scope. Use ALL, PORTFOLIOS, or INSTRUMENTS.

controlsCatalog.name: q
controlsCatalog.description: Optional case-insensitive search text applied to currency code.

controlsCatalog.name: limit
controlsCatalog.description: Maximum number of currency lookup items to return after filtering.

controlsCatalog.name: LOTUS_CORE_CAP_SUPPORT_APIS_ENABLED
controlsCatalog.description: Toggles support overview capability exposure.

controlsCatalog.name: LOTUS_CORE_CAP_LINEAGE_APIS_ENABLED
controlsCatalog.description: Toggles lineage capability exposure.

controlsCatalog.name: LOTUS_CORE_CAP_UPLOAD_APIS_ENABLED
controlsCatalog.description: Toggles bulk upload capability exposure.

controlsCatalog.name: LOTUS_CORE_CAP_SIMULATION_ENABLED
controlsCatalog.description: Toggles simulation capability exposure.

controlsCatalog.name: LOTUS_CORE_CAPABILITY_TENANT_OVERRIDES_JSON
controlsCatalog.description: Tenant-level capability and workflow override policy.

controlsCatalog.name: LOTUS_CORE_POLICY_VERSION
controlsCatalog.description: Policy version surfaced in capabilities and policy metadata.

controlsCatalog.name: LOTUS_CORE_INTEGRATION_SNAPSHOT_POLICY_JSON
controlsCatalog.description: Integration policy controlling allowed sections and strict mode.

endpoints.description: Endpoint that serves Prometheus metrics.
Return process liveness for the service runtime.
Return dependency-aware readiness for the service runtime.

Returns canonical portfolio discovery records with optional filtering by portfolio ID, portfolio identifier list, client grouping ID, and booking center. Use this route for portfolio lookup, selector population, and navigation scope discovery; do not use it as a substitute for single-portfolio detail, workspace composition, or holdings/reporting reads.

endpoints.summary: Get canonical portfolio detail by ID
endpoints.description: Returns the canonical portfolio identity and standing metadata for one portfolio identifier. Use this route when a downstream workflow needs the source-owned portfolio record before composing workspace, holdings, transaction, or reporting reads; do not use it as a substitute for portfolio positions, transaction-ledger, or reporting routes.

endpoints.summary: Get Position History for a Security
endpoints.description: Returns epoch-aware position history for a portfolio-security key across an optional date range. Use this route for holdings drill-down, lineage-aware troubleshooting, and historical security-level state inspection when a downstream consumer needs dated position-history rows; do not use it as a substitute for the strategic latest-holdings read, transaction-ledger rows, or reporting summaries.

endpoints.summary: Get Latest Positions for a Portfolio
endpoints.description:
What: Return the strategic HoldingsAsOf operational read for one portfolio.
How: Resolves current-epoch holdings from daily position snapshots, supplements gaps from position history when snapshot materialization has not caught up yet, and publishes source-data runtime metadata with holdings-level data-quality posture.
When: Use this route for holdings screens, gateway portfolio position books, and other downstream consumers that need the governed source-of-truth holdings surface. Use `as_of_date` for booked historical state on or before a specific business date. Use `include_projected=true` only when the consumer intentionally wants future-dated projected state beyond the latest booked business date. Do not treat this route as a substitute for performance, risk, or reporting-specific aggregation contracts, and do not prefer deprecated reporting convenience shapes when this operational read meets the consumer need.

endpoints.summary: Get strategic cash-account balances for a portfolio
endpoints.description:
What: Return the strategic HoldingsAsOf cash-account balance read for one portfolio.
How: Resolves the latest booked or explicit as-of-date cash positions, preserves source-owned per-account identity, and publishes native, portfolio-currency, and reporting-currency balances with HoldingsAsOf runtime metadata.
When: Use this route when a downstream consumer needs cash-account balances or translated cash totals without rebuilding them from broad holdings state. Prefer this contract for new gateway, advise, or report integrations. Do not use it as a substitute for canonical cash-account master metadata, broad holdings publication, or performance/risk aggregation.

endpoints.summary: Get canonical cash-account master records for a portfolio
endpoints.description: Returns source-owned canonical cash-account master records for one portfolio. Use this endpoint when a downstream consumer needs canonical account identity, currency, role, and lifecycle metadata for operator, support, or source-reference workflows. Do not use this route for per-account balances, translated cash totals, or liquidity analytics; use the strategic cash-balances route when balance publication is required.

endpoints.summary: Get source-owned portfolio liquidity ladder evidence
endpoints.description:
What: Return the PortfolioLiquidityLadder source-data product for one private-banking portfolio.
How: Resolves current cash balances from source holdings, groups non-cash holdings by instrument liquidity tier, and overlays booked plus optional projected settlement-dated cashflows into deterministic cash-availability buckets.
When: Use this route when downstream consumers need source-owned liquidity evidence for portfolio monitoring, DPM supportability, or client reporting. Do not use it as an advice recommendation, OMS execution forecast, tax methodology, best-execution assessment, or predictive market-impact model.

What: Return source-owned portfolio cash movement totals for a bounded cashflow-date window.
How: Aggregates latest cashflow rows by source-owned classification, timing, currency, and portfolio/position flow scope while preserving source-data product metadata.
When: Use this route when downstream outcome, reporting, or DPM consumers need cash movement evidence without recalculating cashflow rows. Do not use it as a cashflow forecast, funding recommendation, treasury instruction, or OMS acknowledgement.

endpoints.summary: Get BUY Lot State for a Position
endpoints.description: Returns durable BUY lot records for a portfolio-security key, including linkage and policy metadata used for lifecycle traceability. Use this endpoint for transaction-state audit, reconciliation, and support investigation when a caller needs the underlying BUY lot ledger; do not use it as a general holdings or reporting read.

Returns accrued-income offset state initialized by BUY events for a portfolio-security key. Use this endpoint for fixed-income audit, income-support, and reconciliation investigation when the caller needs the persisted BUY-side offset ledger; do not use it as a portfolio-income summary route.

Returns security-side BUY linkage fields and linked cashflow details for reconciliation. Use this endpoint for transaction-level audit and settlement investigation when a caller needs the persisted BUY-to-cash linkage; do not use it as a general cash-balance or portfolio-cashflow read.

endpoints.summary: Get SELL Disposal State for a Position
endpoints.description: Returns SELL disposal records for a portfolio-security key, including disposed quantity, disposed cost basis, realized P&L, and policy/linkage metadata for audit and reconciliation. Use this endpoint for transaction-state audit, realized-P&L traceability, and disposal-method investigation when the caller needs persisted SELL disposal rows; do not use it as a general performance, tax-reporting, or holdings read.

Returns security-side SELL linkage fields and linked settlement cashflow details for deterministic reconciliation. Use this endpoint for transaction-level settlement audit when a caller needs the persisted SELL-to-cash linkage; do not use it as a portfolio cashflow, liquidity, or reporting summary route.

What: Return the strategic TransactionLedgerWindow operational read for one portfolio.
How: Publishes the canonical portfolio transaction ledger with date-window filters, instrument/security drill-down, FX and linked-event filters, optional reporting-currency restatement for monetary fields, pagination, and sorting.
When: Use this route when a downstream consumer needs governed transaction-ledger rows rather than summary aggregations. Use `security_id` for holdings drill-down, `instrument_id` for instrument-specific inspection, and FX/event filters such as `component_type`, `linked_transaction_group_id`, `fx_contract_id`, `swap_event_id`, `near_leg_group_id`, or `far_leg_group_id` when the consumer needs multi-row economic event analysis. Use `reporting_currency` when a downstream reporting surface needs ledger rows and field-aware reporting-currency monetary restatement without falling back to deprecated reporting summary routes. Results default to latest-first ordering by `transaction_date` descending unless `sort_by` and `sort_order` are provided explicitly.

What: Return a portfolio-level summary of explicit source-recorded realized tax evidence from booked transaction ledger rows.
How: Aggregates withholding tax and other recorded tax or interest deduction amounts by source currency, optionally restating totals into a requested reporting currency using Core FX evidence.
When: Use this route when downstream consumers need auditable portfolio-level tax evidence without reconstructing tax totals from ledger rows. The response is source evidence only and must not be used as tax advice, after-tax optimization, tax-loss harvesting suitability, jurisdiction-specific recommendation, client-tax approval, tax-reporting certification, or OMS acknowledgement.

What: Return the portfolio-level daily net cashflow projection for operational liquidity planning.
How: Projects daily net cashflow points from the resolved as-of baseline across the requested horizon, with booked-only mode and projected mode for settlement-dated future external cash movements.
When: Use this route when a downstream consumer needs a dedicated cashflow outlook instead of broad portfolio state or performance interpretation. Keep forecasting, performance analytics, and advisory recommendation logic outside this contract.

Returns reference instrument records with optional filtering by security and product type. Use this route for canonical security master lookup, reference-data diagnostics, and thin lookup/enrichment support workflows; do not use it as a substitute for portfolio positions, pricing history, or downstream analytics output.

endpoints.summary: Get market-price series for a security
endpoints.description: Returns market price series for a security with optional date-range filtering. Use this route for source-owned pricing history, valuation checks, and market-data diagnostics; do not use it as a substitute for performance analytics, portfolio valuation outputs, or holdings reads.

endpoints.summary: Get FX-rate series for a currency pair
endpoints.description: Returns FX rates for a currency pair over an optional date range. Use this route for source-owned FX conversion history, valuation conversion checks, and reconciliation diagnostics; do not use it as a substitute for portfolio performance, risk analytics, or derived reporting outputs.

Returns portfolio selector options for lotus-gateway and UI portfolio selection workflows. Use this route for thin selector catalogs only; do not use it as a substitute for canonical portfolio detail or broader portfolio-state reads.

Returns instrument selector options for lotus-gateway and UI trade/intake workflows. Use this route for thin selector catalogs only; do not use it as a substitute for canonical instrument reference reads or enrichment output.

Returns distinct currency selector options derived from portfolio base currencies and instrument currencies. Use this route for selector population only; do not use it as a substitute for FX-rate history or broader market-data contracts.

What: Return source-owned assets-under-management views for a resolved reporting scope.
How: Resolves latest-snapshot or explicit as-of-date holdings totals for a single portfolio, portfolio list, or business unit, with optional reporting-currency restatement.
When: Use this contract when a downstream consumer needs AUM totals and per-portfolio AUM breakdowns rather than broad holdings state or summary composition. Prefer this route over reconstructing AUM from holdings rows when the downstream need is a governed AUM figure.

What: Return the strategic allocation views for a resolved reporting scope.
How: Computes reporting-currency allocation buckets across Lotus-supported classification dimensions such as asset class, currency, sector, country, region, product type, rating, and issuer hierarchy, with explicit look-through capability metadata.
When: Use this contract when a downstream consumer needs allocation buckets rather than broad state publication. Prefer this route over mining allocation views from `core-snapshot` when the need is report-ready or UI-ready allocation analysis.

What: Return the strategic historical portfolio summary for one portfolio and as-of date.
How: Resolves snapshot-backed holdings totals, cash versus invested split, and summary coverage metadata with reporting-currency restatement.
When: Use this contract when UI or reporting consumers need a restated summary in portfolio currency and reporting currency without rebuilding holdings totals client-side. Prefer this route over downstream reconstruction from holdings rows or `core-snapshot` when the consumer needs summary figures rather than sectioned source-state payloads. This is the correct lotus-core summary seam for report-ready wealth totals; it should not absorb performance, risk, or narrative reporting ownership.
Endpoint that serves Prometheus metrics.
Return process liveness for the service runtime.
Return dependency-aware readiness for the service runtime.

What: Accept canonical portfolio master records.
How: Validate portfolio schema, enforce idempotency/mode checks, and publish asynchronously for persistence.
When: Use when onboarding or updating portfolio metadata from upstream systems.

What: Accept one canonical transaction record for ledger ingestion.
How: Validate contract, enforce mode and rate controls, propagate any idempotency key as publish lineage, then publish asynchronously to Kafka.
When: Use for low-volume operational corrections or single-record onboarding.

What: Accept a batch of canonical transaction records.
How: Persist ingestion job metadata, validate payload, and publish all valid records asynchronously.
When: Use for standard API-driven batch ingestion workflows.

What: Accept canonical instrument/security reference records.
How: Validate schema, enforce ingestion mode/idempotency controls, and publish to asynchronous persistence pipeline.
When: Use for security master onboarding and reference data corrections.

What: Accept canonical market price observations for securities.
How: Validate payload, enforce ingestion guardrails, and publish asynchronous events for valuation processing.
When: Use for daily close pricing loads or intraday approved market data updates.

What: Accept canonical foreign-exchange rate observations.
How: Validate FX rate contract, enforce ingestion controls, and publish asynchronous events for downstream valuation.
When: Use for scheduled FX reference updates and approved manual corrections.

What: Accept canonical business calendar dates used by valuation and processing lifecycles.
How: Validate date records, apply ingestion controls, and publish asynchronous persistence events.
When: Use for calendar setup, holiday updates, and date-correction operations.

What: Accept transaction identifiers that require deterministic historical recalculation.
How: Validate request, persist ingestion job metadata, and publish reprocessing command events.
When: Use for operational correction workflows after retroactive data changes.

What: Accept a mixed onboarding bundle containing portfolio, instrument, transaction, market-price, FX-rate, and business-date records.
How: Validate adapter payload and fan out records into existing canonical ingestion topics.
When: Use for adapter-mode onboarding (UI/manual/file workflows), not primary upstream integration.

endpoints.summary: Preview and validate bulk upload data
endpoints.description:
What: Validate CSV/XLSX ingestion payloads without publishing records.
How: Parse file rows, apply entity-specific schema checks, and return row-level validation feedback.
When: Use before commit to catch data-quality issues in bulk adapter uploads.

What: Commit CSV/XLSX data into canonical ingestion topics.
How: Validate rows, enforce mode controls, and publish valid records (optionally partial when allow_partial=true).
When: Use after preview passes for adapter-mode bulk ingestion.

What: Accept effective-dated benchmark assignment records for portfolios.
How: Validate canonical contract, enforce ingestion controls, and upsert durable records.
When: Use for benchmark onboarding, assignment updates, and restatement correction cycles.

What: Accept approved model portfolio master/version records for discretionary mandate management.
How: Validate canonical model identity, approval, effective-date, lineage, and quality fields, then upsert durable versioned records.
When: Use when the investment office publishes a new model portfolio version, approval update, retirement, or correction required by lotus-manage stateful DPM.

What: Accept effective-dated instrument target rows for approved model portfolio versions.
How: Validate instrument-level target weights and optional min/max bands, preserve source lineage, and upsert deterministic target rows.
When: Use after model portfolio approval or correction so lotus-manage can resolve DPM target allocation from governed core source data instead of local fallback truth.

What: Accept effective-dated DPM instrument eligibility, product shelf, restriction, liquidity, and settlement profiles.
How: Validate eligibility flags and source lineage, then upsert durable records keyed by security_id, effective_from, and eligibility_version.
When: Use when product shelf, compliance restriction, or settlement master data changes so lotus-manage can build DPM shelf inputs from governed core source data.

What: Accept effective-dated portfolio-to-mandate/model/policy bindings for discretionary portfolio management.
How: Validate authority status, model and policy selectors, rebalance constraints, lineage, and quality fields, then upsert durable binding records.
When: Use when mandate administration, investment policy, or model assignment systems publish a new discretionary authority binding or correction required by lotus-manage stateful DPM.

What: Accept effective-dated client and mandate restriction records for DPM construction.
How: Validate bounded restriction scopes, source lineage, effective dates, and quality fields, then upsert durable restriction profile records.
When: Use when mandate administration, suitability, compliance, or client preference systems publish restriction changes required by lotus-manage stateful DPM.

What: Accept effective-dated client sustainability preference records for DPM construction.
How: Validate bounded framework codes, allocation bounds, exclusions, positive tilts, source lineage, effective dates, and quality fields, then upsert durable preference profile records.
When: Use when client preference, suitability, or sustainability policy systems publish changes required by lotus-manage stateful DPM.

What: Accept effective-dated source-owned client tax profile records for DPM evidence.
How: Validate bounded tax-status, jurisdiction, rate, treaty, source lineage, effective dates, and quality fields, then upsert durable tax profile records.
When: Use when bank tax, client master, or mandate systems publish tax-reference facts needed by lotus-manage. This endpoint stores reference evidence only and does not publish tax advice or after-tax optimization decisions.

What: Accept effective-dated source-owned client tax rule references for DPM evidence.
How: Validate bounded rule category, jurisdiction, applicability, thresholds, rates, source lineage, effective dates, and quality fields, then upsert durable rule-set records.
When: Use when bank tax or rules systems publish governed tax-reference facts needed by lotus-manage. This endpoint stores reference evidence only and does not approve client tax outcomes, tax-loss harvesting, or jurisdiction-specific recommendations.

What: Accept source-owned client income-needs schedule records for DPM evidence.
How: Validate bounded need type, active window, amount, currency, cadence, source lineage, and quality fields, then upsert durable income-needs schedule records.
When: Use when bank planning or reference systems publish client income-needs facts. This endpoint stores planning evidence only and does not provide financial-planning advice, funding recommendations, suitability approval, or OMS instructions.

What: Accept source-owned liquidity reserve requirement records for DPM evidence.
How: Validate bounded reserve type, required amount, horizon, policy source, effective dates, source lineage, and quality fields, then upsert durable reserve requirements.
When: Use when bank policy, planning, or reference systems publish reserve facts. This endpoint stores evidence only and does not provide funding advice, liquidity advice, client suitability approval, or OMS acknowledgement.

What: Accept source-owned planned withdrawal schedule records for DPM evidence.
How: Validate bounded withdrawal type, scheduled date, amount, currency, source lineage, and quality fields, then upsert durable withdrawal schedule records.
When: Use when bank planning or reference systems publish planned withdrawal facts. This endpoint stores evidence only and does not forecast cashflows, recommend funding, approve client advice, or acknowledge OMS execution.

What: Accept benchmark master definition records.
How: Validate canonical contract and upsert effective-dated benchmark metadata.
When: Use for benchmark master onboarding and lifecycle updates.

What: Accept benchmark composition time-series records.
How: Validate composition weights and upsert effective-dated component memberships.
When: Use for benchmark rebalance and composition history maintenance.

What: Accept index master definition records.
How: Validate canonical contract and upsert effective-dated index metadata.
When: Use for index onboarding and attribution metadata lifecycle updates.

What: Accept raw index price series records.
How: Validate canonical contract and upsert time-series observations.
When: Use for daily market close processing and historical backfills.

What: Accept raw vendor-provided index return series records.
How: Validate the canonical record contract and upsert deterministic time-series rows.
When: Use when upstream publishes return series directly.

What: Accept raw vendor-provided benchmark return series records.
How: Validate the canonical record contract and upsert deterministic time-series rows.
When: Use when benchmark return series is provided by upstream vendor.

What: Accept risk-free reference series records.
How: Validate convention fields and upsert deterministic time-series rows.
When: Use for benchmark-relative analytics and risk-adjusted metric inputs.

What: Accept classification taxonomy records used for attribution alignment.
How: Validate canonical dimensions and upsert effective-dated taxonomy entries.
When: Use when platform taxonomy labels are introduced or updated.

What: Accept canonical cash-account master records.
How: Validate PB/WM cash-account identity and upsert durable portfolio/account linkage metadata.
When: Use during onboarding and cash-account lifecycle maintenance.

What: Accept effective-dated look-through composition rows for funds and structured products.
How: Validate component weights and upsert durable decomposition records.
When: Use when downstream allocation consumers need source-owned look-through views.
